text:
There once was a boy named Timmy who loved horses. Every weekend, he'd go to the farm owned by his great-grandmother. The majestic silos gleamed against the sky, and the pasture was always alive with birds.

One particularly warm summer day, as Timmy trotted home through the field, he spotted something incredible: a huge owl, silent as death, perched atop an old elm tree. Its feathers were a haunting ivory color, and timothy drops hung from its wings.

That night, Timmy dreamed of talking birds who gifted him an ancient book. They told him about a powerful sorcerer who kept the owl guard. Heartened, Timmy vowed to undo whatever harm this guardian might cause.

In his courage, he visited the sorcerer early the next morning. Bowls of shining oats! A feast fit for an owl! Seeing Timmy, the sorcerer just smirked. "You want to compete? Fine. But nothing good will come of it."
